Question: For the determinant $$\left |\begin{matrix}1 &  &  & -1 &  &  & 4 \\ 0 &  &  & 2 &  &  & -1 \\ -2 &  &  & -3 &  &  & 3\end{matrix}\right | ,$$ find and then evaluate the minor of ⓐ $$a_{1}$$ ⓑ $$b_{2}$$ ⓒ $$c_{3} .$$

For the determinant $$\left |\begin{matrix}-2 &  &  & -1 &  &  & 0 \\ 3 &  &  & 0 &  &  & -1 \\ -1 &  &  & -2 &  &  & 3\end{matrix}\right | ,$$ find and then evaluate the minor of ⓐ $$a_{2}$$ ⓑ $$b_{3}$$ ⓒ $$c_{2} .$$
Evaluate the determinant $$\left |\begin{matrix}3 &  &  & -2 &  &  & 4 \\ 0 &  &  & -1 &  &  & -2 \\ 2 &  &  & 3 &  &  & -1\end{matrix}\right | ,$$ by expanding by minors along the first row.
Evaluate the determinant $$\left |\begin{matrix}3 &  &  & -2 &  &  & -2 \\ 2 &  &  & -1 &  &  & 4 \\ -1 &  &  & 0 &  &  & -3\end{matrix}\right | ,$$ by expanding by minors along the first row.
Evaluate the determinant $$\left |\begin{matrix}2 &  &  & -1 &  &  & -3 \\ 0 &  &  & 3 &  &  & -4 \\ 3 &  &  & -4 &  &  & -3\end{matrix}\right |$$ by expanding by minors.
Evaluate the determinant $$\left |\begin{matrix}-2 &  &  & -1 &  &  & -3 \\ -1 &  &  & 2 &  &  & 2 \\ 4 &  &  & -4 &  &  & 0\end{matrix}\right |$$ by expanding by minors.
Solve using Cramer’s rule: $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}3 x + y = -3 \\ 2 x + 3 y = 6\end{matrix}\right . .$$
Solve using Cramer’s rule: $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}\text{unicode[:-]} x + y = 2 \\ 2 x + y = -4\end{matrix}\right . .$$
Solve the system of equations using Cramer’s Rule: $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}3 x + 8 y + 2 z = -5 \\ 2 x + 5 y - 3 z = 0 \\ x + 2 y - 2 z = -1\end{matrix}\right . .$$
Solve the system of equations using Cramer’s Rule: $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}3 x + y - 6 z = -3 \\ 2 x + 6 y + 3 z = 0 \\ 3 x + 2 y - 3 z = -6\end{matrix}\right . .$$
Solve the system of equations using Cramer’s rule: $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}4 x - 3 y = 8 \\ 8 x - 6 y = 14\end{matrix}\right . .$$
Solve the system of equations using Cramer’s rule: $$\{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}x = -3 y + 4 \\ 2 x + 6 y = 8\end{matrix}\right . .$$
Determine whether the points $$( 3 , -2 ) ,$$ $$( 5 , -3 ) ,$$ and $$( 1 , -1 )$$ are collinear.
Determine whether the points $$( -4 , -1 ) ,$$ $$( -6 , 2 ) ,$$ and $$( -2 , -4 )$$ are collinear.
